paper.paper_title: 114 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. A         is flying over the houses.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   Look at the picture. A         is flying over the houses.
     (A) bird
     (B) butterfly
     (C) kite
     (D) plane

questions.explanation: 圖片中飛行於房屋上方的物品，依題幹「flying over the houses」+ 一般日常常見最合理的是「a plane（飛機）」，故 (D) 正確。bird、butterfly 雖能飛但不會被一般描述為「houses」上方意象的主體；kite 通常綁線且需強調「fly a kite」。

questions.question_text: When I was a teenager, I was very          .  But now, it’s easier for me to talk to people.

source_meta.raw_text:
2. 	 When I was a teenager, I was very          .  But now, it’s easier for me to talk to people.
     (A) happy            (B) lazy                 (C) popular             (D) shy

questions.explanation: 上下文「現在我比較容易跟人講話」反推青少年期的相反狀態，「shy（害羞）」最切合，故 (D) 正確。(A) happy 與「easier to talk to people」無對立；(B) lazy、(C) popular 都無法解釋現在反而較易交流。

questions.question_text: Lena doesn’t want to go         with John because she is afraid of water.

source_meta.raw_text:
3. 	 Lena doesn’t want to go         with John because she is afraid of water.
     (A) dancing           (B) hiking            (C) sailing            (D) shopping

questions.explanation: 「她怕水（afraid of water）」對應的水上活動是「sailing（駕船）」，故 (C) 正確。(A) dancing、(B) hiking、(D) shopping 與水無關。

questions.question_text: Cindy enjoys         her dad read stories to her before bed.

source_meta.raw_text:
4. 	 Cindy enjoys         her dad read stories to her before bed.
     (A) to listen to      (B) listening to        (C) listen to           (D) listens to

questions.explanation: 「enjoy + V-ing」是固定搭配，故 (B) listening to 正確。(A) to listen to 為不定詞、(C) listen to 為原型、(D) listens to 為第三人稱單數，皆不合 enjoy 後接動名詞的文法規則。

questions.question_text: Dad is busy cooking in the kitchen.  Dinner will be            in ten minutes.

source_meta.raw_text:
5. 	 Dad is busy cooking in the kitchen.  Dinner will be            in ten minutes.
     (A) free             (B) full               (C) medium                 (D) ready

questions.explanation: Dad 正在廚房煮飯、十分鐘後晚餐將「準備好」，「ready」最切合，故 (D) 正確。(A) free（免費的）、(B) full（飽的）、(C) medium（中等）皆與「即將上桌」語意不合。

questions.question_text:
There are so many new          in the office.  It’ll take me some time to remember who is
who.

source_meta.raw_text:
6. 	 There are so many new          in the office.  It’ll take me some time to remember who is
     who.
     (A) faces            (B) ideas                (C) rules              (D) tools

questions.explanation: 辦公室裡許多新來的事物 + 需要時間記住誰是誰，最合理的是新「faces（面孔/同事）」，故 (A) 正確。(B) ideas、(C) rules、(D) tools 與「記住誰是誰」無對應。

questions.question_text:
I feel like a       .  I was looking for my keys for hours but they have been in my pocket
the whole time.

source_meta.raw_text:
7. 	 I feel like a       .  I was looking for my keys for hours but they have been in my pocket
     the whole time.
     (A) fool               (B) ghost               (C) king               (D) stranger

questions.explanation: 找鑰匙找了好幾小時、結果一直在自己口袋裡，最符合「I feel like a fool（覺得自己像個笨蛋）」的情境，故 (A) 正確。(B) ghost、(C) king、(D) stranger 都不符合自嘲糊塗的語意。

questions.question_text:
Mr. and Mrs. Wu have three daughters.  Two are in high school, and        is in
elementary school.

source_meta.raw_text:
8. 	 Mr. and Mrs. Wu have three daughters.  Two are in high school, and        is in
     elementary school.
     (A) each           (B) the other        (C) the one          (D) the next

questions.explanation: 三個女兒，「Two are…and the other is…」是「三者中其餘一個」的固定用法，故 (B) the other 正確。(A) each 用於整體一一指；(C) the one 缺乏對比；(D) the next 表「下一個」非「剩下那一個」。

questions.question_text: It is hard for trees to       along this beach because of the strong winds from the sea.

source_meta.raw_text:
9. 	 It is hard for trees to       along this beach because of the strong winds from the sea.
     (A) blow                (B) build             (C) follow             (D) grow

questions.explanation: 強風使樹木難以「生長（grow）」於海邊，故 (D) 正確。(A) blow 是風的動作；(B) build 不用於樹；(C) follow 與樹無關。

questions.question_text: Christmas            and I want to visit my aunt abroad.  Do you have any plans yet?

source_meta.raw_text:
10. Christmas            and I want to visit my aunt abroad.  Do you have any plans yet?
    (A) came                (B) comes              (C) is coming         (D) was coming

questions.explanation: 「Christmas is coming」表示聖誕節即將到來，搭配下文「我想去拜訪阿姨」屬計劃中事件，現在進行式表近未來最自然，故 (C) 正確。(A)(B)(D) 時態不符「即將到來且尚在計畫中」的語境。

questions.question_text: Jo won’t be happy if you’re late for his party tonight, so      sure that you arrive on time.

source_meta.raw_text:
11.   Jo won’t be happy if you’re late for his party tonight, so      sure that you arrive on time.  
      (A) make              (B) makes                (C) to make          (D) is making

questions.explanation: 前句已用助動詞 won't 假設條件、後句以對等連接 so 接祈使句「make sure that you arrive on time」，動詞應用原型，故 (A) make 正確。(B)(C)(D) 形式皆不符祈使句結構。

questions.question_text:
You may have a long drive because of the terrible       .  There are usually a lot of cars
and buses during this time.

source_meta.raw_text:
12. You may have a long drive because of the terrible       .  There are usually a lot of cars
    and buses during this time.
    (A) experience         (B) machine          (C) service           (D) traffic

questions.explanation: 「a long drive because of the terrible…」+「a lot of cars and buses」兩條線索都指向交通問題，故 (D) traffic 正確。(A) experience、(B) machine、(C) service 皆無法搭配「因 X 而開很久的車」。

questions.question_text:
In the future, there will         be greater basketball players than Stephen Curry, but now
we believe he is the best!

source_meta.raw_text:
13. In the future, there will         be greater basketball players than Stephen Curry, but now
    we believe he is the best!
    (A) again               (B) already            (C) never              (D) perhaps

questions.explanation: 句意「未來會有更偉大的球員，但現在我們相信他是最棒的」，前句用助動詞 will + 副詞，「perhaps（也許）」表示可能性，最切合「未來不確定但現在認定」的語氣，故 (D) 正確。(A) again、(B) already、(C) never 都不合上下文邏輯。

questions.question_text:
I guess the rainwater has come in from the kitchen.  See?           of the windows are closed
except the one in the kitchen.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. I guess the rainwater has come in from the kitchen.  See?           of the windows are closed
    except the one in the kitchen.
    (A) All                (B) Both              (C) Most                 (D) Some

questions.explanation: 下文「除了廚房那扇」即所有窗戶都關了，「All of the windows are closed except the one」最自然，故 (A) All 正確。(B) Both 限兩扇；(C) Most 不能搭配 except + 單一窗戶；(D) Some 與「除了一扇外都關」邏輯不合。

questions.question_text:
machines have been used to pick fruits for a long time, they were not used on
strawberry farms until several years ago.

source_meta.raw_text:
15.	        machines have been used to pick fruits for a long time, they were not used on
    strawberry farms until several years ago.
    (A) Although          (B) Because          (C) Before              (D) If

questions.explanation: 前後子句語意對立（採收水果的機器使用已久 vs. 草莓農場才剛開始用），需轉折連接詞，故 (A) Although 正確。(B) Because、(C) Before、(D) If 皆不適合表達讓步轉折。

questions.question_text:
There are online videos that teach you exercises you can do at home.  They’ll            you a
trip to the gym, and some money too.

source_meta.raw_text:
16. There are online videos that teach you exercises you can do at home.  They’ll            you a
    trip to the gym, and some money too.
    (A) cost               (B) give              (C) keep             (D) save

questions.explanation: 「線上運動影片可省去你上健身房的行程，也省下一些錢」，「save you a trip + save money」是固定用法，故 (D) save 正確。(A) cost 是花費；(B) give、(C) keep 與省去成本的語意不合。

questions.question_text:
Jane’s parents are always happy to see their grandchildren, but mine        less so when I
visit them with my kids.

source_meta.raw_text:
17.   Jane’s parents are always happy to see their grandchildren, but mine        less so when I
      visit them with my kids.
      (A) is                 (B) are                (C) do               (D) does

questions.explanation: 主詞 mine（= my parents）為複數，故動詞用 are，回應前句「are always happy」的對應結構，故 (B) are 正確。(A) is 為單數；(C) do、(D) does 不適合形容詞 less so 的補語。

questions.question_text: Before she           about it, you should tell Daphne you broke her favorite cup.

source_meta.raw_text:
18. Before she           about it, you should tell Daphne you broke her favorite cup.
    (A) asks                (B) asked               (C) was asking        (D) will ask

questions.explanation: 副詞子句 before 的時間從句，當主句使用未來語意（you should tell）時，從句用一般現在式表示未來，故 (A) asks 正確。(B) asked 為過去式；(C) was asking 為過去進行式；(D) will ask 在 before 子句中不能用未來式。

questions.question_text:
It was very windy this morning.   Some of the shirts on the balcony were blown away
in the pond.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. It was very windy this morning.   Some of the shirts on the balcony were blown away
             in the pond.
    (A) fell              (B) and fell       (C) fallen            (D) and fallen

questions.explanation: 前句主動語態「Some of the shirts...were blown away」，後句以對等連接詞並列同主詞動詞，需「and fell」連接過去式動詞，故 (B) and fell 正確。(A) fell 缺連接詞；(C)(D) 形式不正確。

media.passage_text:
Rex is a dog that lives at the bakery next to our school.  He is cute and friendly.
Every morning, he stands in front of the school to welcome everybody.  We all see Rex
as one of us.  But one Monday morning, I was surprised that Rex was not there to say
hello to us.  “Rex is in the hospital.  He was hit by a car last night,” my classmate told me.
We went to see Rex in the hospital that day after school.  Two weeks later, Rex was much
better, and we decided to take him for a walk every evening.  Because of the exercise, Rex
became healthier and stronger.  Soon he could happily run and jump again.  He is now as
handsome and strong as before!

questions.question_text: What happened to Rex?

source_meta.raw_text:
20. What happened to Rex?
    (A) He got lost.
    (B) He got hurt.
    (C) He bit people.
    (D) He ate too much.

questions.explanation: 原文「He was hit by a car last night」直接說明 Rex 受傷，故 (B) He got hurt 正確。(A) 沒有走失；(C) 沒咬人；(D) 沒提吃太多。

questions.question_text: How did the writer help Rex?
options: By taking him to see a doctor.
By looking for a new home for him.

source_meta.raw_text:
21.     How did the writer help Rex?
        (A) By calling the police.
        (B) By making him exercise.
        (C) By taking him to see a doctor.
        (D) By looking for a new home for him.

questions.explanation: 原文「we decided to take him for a walk every evening. Because of the exercise, Rex became healthier and stronger」直接呼應 (B) By making him exercise，故 (B) 正確。(A)(C)(D) 文中皆未對應。

questions.question_text: What do we know about Mark from the dialogue?
options: He is looking for a new job.
He did not like Linda’s cake.
He is getting married to Jenny.

source_meta.raw_text:
22. What do we know about Mark from the dialogue?
    (A) He made Linda unhappy.
    (B) He is looking for a new job.
    (C) He did not like Linda’s cake.
    (D) He is getting married to Jenny.

questions.explanation: 本題搭配對話內容（pdftotext 未抽到完整對話）。從選項配置與答案 (A) He made Linda unhappy 推測，對話應呈現 Mark 做了某件事讓 Linda 不快的情境，故 (A) 正確。其餘選項屬無關訊息。

questions.question_text: Which is most likely an example of stealing someone＇     s thunder?           likely 可能
options: Dennis never changes his mind except when his wife tells him to.
Melisa tells Tom she’ll go to the party but tells her mom she’ll stay home.
Jeff tells everyone he’ll move abroad when Ivy is still telling them about her baby.
Alisa says she doesn’t care what we have for lunch but also doesn’t like the restaurant we chose.

source_meta.raw_text:
23. Which is most likely an example of stealing someone＇     s thunder?           likely 可能
    (A) Dennis never changes his mind except when his wife tells him to.
    (B) Melisa tells Tom she’ll go to the party but tells her mom she’ll stay home.
    (C) Jeff tells everyone he’ll move abroad when Ivy is still telling them about her baby.
    (D) Alisa says she doesn’t care what we have for lunch but also doesn’t like the restaurant
        we chose.

questions.explanation: 「stealing someone's thunder」意為在他人正受關注時搶走鋒頭。(C) Jeff 在 Ivy 還在分享自己孩子消息時宣布「我要搬到國外」，搶走 Ivy 的關注焦點，最符合此習語，故 (C) 正確。(A) 是聽從妻子；(B) 是說謊；(D) 是被動接受。

media.passage_text:
Buy a White Lake City Card When You’re in the City
 With any of the Cards, you can
- visit the city by metro, bus, or train as many times as you want. *
- take one child under 12 with you for free.
- save 20% on tickets to public museums.
 The 1-day, 3-day, and 5-day Cards are for trips from Monday to Friday, and the
Weekend Card is for trips on weekends and holidays.
 Check the map and buy the right Card for the zones you’ll be entering.
Card    Zone 1 Zones 1-2          Zones 1-3
1-day     $20           $40         $60
3-day     $40           $60         $80
5-day     $50           $80         $110
Weekend        $30           $50         $80
* The Cards are only for trips inside the three zones.
ⓐ White Lake Main Station
ⓑ Museum of White Lake City History
ⓒ Museum of Modern Art
ⓓ White Lake
ⓔ White Lake Castle
ⓕ White Lake Airport
 zone 區

questions.question_text: What can you do with a White Lake City Card?
options: Visit any public museum in the city for free.
Take a train to places out of the three zones.
Move around the city by metro as much as you want.

source_meta.raw_text:
24. What can you do with a White Lake City Card?
    (A) Save 20% on children’s train tickets.   
    (B) Visit any public museum in the city for free.  
    (C) Take a train to places out of the three zones.  
    (D) Move around the city by metro as much as you want.

questions.explanation: 原文「visit the city by metro, bus, or train as many times as you want」明白指可無限搭乘公共運輸，故 (D) Move around the city by metro as much as you want 正確。(A) 八折是博物館門票而非兒童火車；(B) 博物館是 20% 折扣不是免費；(C) 卡只能在三個 zone 內使用。

questions.question_text:
Stacy is going to White Lake City and is staying at a hotel near the White Lake Main
Station.  She wants to visit the Museum of White Lake City History on Friday and see
White Lake on Saturday.  If she plans to buy (a) White Lake City Card(s), which of the four
choices will be best for her and cost her the least?
options: A 1-day Card for Zone 1 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.
A 1-day Card for Zones 1-2 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. Stacy is going to White Lake City and is staying at a hotel near the White Lake Main
    Station.  She wants to visit the Museum of White Lake City History on Friday and see
    White Lake on Saturday.  If she plans to buy (a) White Lake City Card(s), which of the four
    choices will be best for her and cost her the least?
    (A) A 3-day Card for Zone 1.
    (B) A Weekend Card for Zones 1-3.
    (C) A 1-day Card for Zone 1 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.
    (D) A 1-day Card for Zones 1-2 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.

questions.explanation: Stacy 週五要去歷史博物館（Zone 1，主車站附近）+ 週六去 White Lake（在 Zones 1-2）。週五用 1-day for Zone 1（$20）+ 週末用 Weekend Card for Zones 1-2（$50）= $70 是涵蓋目的地的最便宜組合，故 (C) 正確。(A) 3-day Zone 1 不含週六也不含 White Lake；(B) Weekend 1-3 不含週五；(D) 1-day Zones 1-2 多花錢且 Friday 只需 Zone 1。

media.passage_text:
At 5 o’clock every morning, while most people are sleeping,
16-year-old Libby Larson is already up.  She mops the floor, gets
the mail and the newspapers, and gets ready for visitors to Rolling
Acres.   Libby’s grandparents started Rolling Acres in 1970, and
since then all of the Larson family have been working there.
Rolling Acres used to be just a sheep farm, but now, with its fruit
trees, vegetable gardens, and many other kinds of animals, it has
become a popular place for families to go on vacation.  Visitors can
pick fruits, collect eggs, and feed the animals.  It is one of Libby’s
jobs to show kids how to feed the baby sheep.
For her work, Libby is paid $10 an hour.  This summer, she expects to make about $1,200
and plans to buy a cell phone.  Her friends think it’s great to make that much money in a
summer.  However, for Libby, working at a family business also means making sacrifices.
“I’d love to sleep until noon or go to summer camp like other kids.  My friends stopped
inviting me on trips because I’m always busy in the summer.”  However, Libby also said,
“But it’s better to see both sides.  Working with your family is great.  You feel closer to
each other.”

questions.question_text: What is Rolling Acres?

source_meta.raw_text:
26. What is Rolling Acres?
    (A) A zoo.
    (B) A campground.
    (C) A vacation farm.
    (D) A family restaurant.

questions.explanation: 原文「Rolling Acres used to be just a sheep farm, but now…it has become a popular place for families to go on vacation」直接點出是供家庭度假的農場，故 (C) A vacation farm 正確。(A) 不是動物園；(B) 不是露營場；(D) 不是餐廳。

questions.question_text: What do we learn from the first paragraph?                             paragraph 段落
options: What Libby does at Rolling Acres.
What visitors think of Rolling Acres.
Why Libby’s grandparents started Rolling Acres.
What the Larson family’s plans are for Rolling Acres.

source_meta.raw_text:
27. What do we learn from the first paragraph?                             paragraph 段落
    (A) What Libby does at Rolling Acres.
    (B) What visitors think of Rolling Acres.
    (C) Why Libby’s grandparents started Rolling Acres.
    (D) What the Larson family’s plans are for Rolling Acres.

questions.explanation: 第一段描述 Libby 早起、拖地、收信件、迎接遊客等工作內容，主軸即「Libby 在 Rolling Acres 做什麼」，故 (A) 正確。(B) 文中未呈現訪客觀感；(C) 祖父母創立原因僅一句帶過；(D) 未談未來計畫。

questions.question_text: What does making sacrifices mean?
options: Learning to make money and use it wisely.
Getting to know different sides of your family.
Making excuses for failing to do something difficult.
Giving up something important to do something else.

source_meta.raw_text:
28. What does making sacrifices mean?
    (A) Learning to make money and use it wisely.
    (B) Getting to know different sides of your family.
    (C) Making excuses for failing to do something difficult.
    (D) Giving up something important to do something else.

questions.explanation: Libby 引文「I'd love to sleep until noon or go to summer camp like other kids…My friends stopped inviting me on trips」清楚說明她為了家業放棄了想做的事，故 (D) Giving up something important to do something else 正確。(A) 只談賺錢；(B) 認識家人非主旨；(C) 找藉口與 sacrifice 反向。

media.passage_text:
Around the year 400, people came to Easter Island.
So many trees here!
In their free time, they loved
They used trees to make fire and                  making statues with big rocks. With
build houses. Because of the trees,               the trees they cut down, they could
people had a great life on the island.            move the statues around easily.
Trees were needed and used in every part of their life.
Years passed, and there were fewer and fewer trees on Easter Island.
Without enough trees to keep                                 And I’m hungry.
water under the ground, the land              I’m thirsty.
became dry, and plants didn’t grow.
People began to fight for water and food. The statues were moved to fighting
grounds to show their power. The last trees were probably cut down at this time.
Those who cut down                               Let’s not make Earth, our only home,
the last trees surely                            another Easter Island.
understood how
important trees were
to them—but still,
they did it. When the
last trees fell to the
ground, people on the
island fell, too.

questions.question_text: What do the comics tell us?
options: Follow the old ways of life.
Save our planet before it’s too late.
Treat others the way we want to be treated.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. What do the comics tell us?
    (A) Enjoy life while we can.
    (B) Follow the old ways of life.
    (C) Save our planet before it’s too late.
    (D) Treat others the way we want to be treated.

questions.explanation: 漫畫從「樹木充足→過度砍伐→生態崩潰→人類滅絕」最後以「Let's not make Earth, our only home, another Easter Island」作結，呼籲珍惜地球資源，故 (C) Save our planet before it's too late 正確。(A) 即時行樂與警示主旨相反；(B) 守舊；(D) 待人之道皆與環境議題無關。

questions.question_text: What can we learn about the people in the comics?
options: They made fire before starting a fight.
They fought for land and plants all the time.
They prayed to the statues beside a large fire.
They used statues to show how strong they were.

source_meta.raw_text:
30. What can we learn about the people in the comics?
    (A) They made fire before starting a fight.
    (B) They fought for land and plants all the time.
    (C) They prayed to the statues beside a large fire.
    (D) They used statues to show how strong they were.

questions.explanation: 原文「The statues were moved to fighting grounds to show their power」直接說明石像被移到爭奪場以展示力量，故 (D) They used statues to show how strong they were 正確。(A) 文未提先升火再戰鬥；(B) 是為水與食物而戰非土地與植物；(C) 未提祭拜。

questions.question_text: What does did it in Picture 7 mean?
options: Move the statues to fighting grounds.

source_meta.raw_text:
31.    What does did it in Picture 7 mean?
       (A) Fall to the ground.
       (B) Cut down the last trees.
       (C) Move the statues to fighting grounds.
       (D) Understand how important trees were.

questions.explanation: Picture 7 緊接「Those who cut down the last trees surely understood…—but still, they did it」，「did it」即指砍下最後的樹，故 (B) Cut down the last trees 正確。(A) fall 是後文人類的隱喻；(C)(D) 與時序及文意不合。

media.passage_text:
Many years ago, Dan Ariely bought a cabinet from IKEA.  IKEA is a
famous furniture store that sells boxes of furniture parts.  People who
buy them have to put all the parts together themselves.  It took Ariely hours to build
his cabinet.  Ariely didn’t really enjoy himself when he was trying to put all the parts
together.  However, after he finished, he felt quite good about himself and his cabinet.
Later, he found that he loved his cabinet more than any of his other furniture.
Ariely wanted to know if anyone shared his feelings, so he did
a fun origami study.  He invited two groups of people to join his
study.  He called one group “builders” and the other group “buyers.”
Builders were told to make origami, like paper frogs or paper birds.  When they
finished, they were asked how much they would spend on their works.  Ariely then
showed the origami to buyers and asked them how much they would pay for them.
The two groups gave very different answers.  Though buyers would pay only five
cents for builders’ origami, builders would spend 25 cents!  Interestingly, builders
believed others would think their works were good enough to cost that much money!
Ariely’s study showed that he was not alone in his IKEA experience — we just love
things more, sometimes blindly, when we make them ourselves.  Ariely called it “the
IKEA effect.”
 effect 效應          furniture 家具

questions.question_text: What did Ariely try to find out in the origami study?
options: If he could stop the IKEA effect.
Why IKEA furniture is so famous.
If anyone shared his IKEA experience.

source_meta.raw_text:
32. What did Ariely try to find out in the origami study?
    (A) If he could stop the IKEA effect.
    (B) Why people love making origami.
    (C) Why IKEA furniture is so famous.
    (D) If anyone shared his IKEA experience.

questions.explanation: 原文「Ariely wanted to know if anyone shared his feelings, so he did a fun origami study」直接點出實驗目的是驗證「是否有人和我（Ariely 對 IKEA 的）感受相同」，故 (D) If anyone shared his IKEA experience 正確。(A) 沒有要阻止 IKEA 效應；(B)(C) 都非實驗主旨。

questions.question_text: Which is true about the origami study?
options: Buyers would spend less on builders’ origami than builders would.
Builders and buyers needed to decide a price on the origami they made.
Builders knew others would not pay as much for the origami as they would.
Buyers would spend more on builders’ origami after knowing how they were made.

source_meta.raw_text:
33. Which is true about the origami study?
    (A) Buyers would spend less on builders’ origami than builders would.
    (B) Builders and buyers needed to decide a price on the origami they made.
    (C) Builders knew others would not pay as much for the origami as they would.
    (D) Buyers would spend more on builders’ origami after knowing how they were made.

questions.explanation: 原文 builders 願花 25 cents、buyers 只願花 5 cents，正是「buyers 出價低於 builders」，故 (A) Buyers would spend less on builders' origami than builders would 正確。(B) 買家不需自己定價；(C) builders 反而以為他人會認可高價；(D) 文中未討論「知道製作過程後」的影響。

questions.question_text:
Jerry just can’t get his daughter Mia to eat more vegetables at dinner.  Every time he tries
to do so, there is always a lot of shouting and crying.  If Jerry wants to have Mia eat more
vegetables by using the IKEA effect, what should he do?
options: Tell Mia that he cooks the vegetables just for her.
Ask Mia to help him cook vegetables for her meal.
Give Mia her favorite candy after she eats vegetables.
Ask Mia what vegetables she likes and cook them for her.

source_meta.raw_text:
34. Jerry just can’t get his daughter Mia to eat more vegetables at dinner.  Every time he tries
    to do so, there is always a lot of shouting and crying.  If Jerry wants to have Mia eat more
    vegetables by using the IKEA effect, what should he do?
    (A) Tell Mia that he cooks the vegetables just for her.
    (B) Ask Mia to help him cook vegetables for her meal.
    (C) Give Mia her favorite candy after she eats vegetables.
    (D) Ask Mia what vegetables she likes and cook them for her.

questions.explanation: IKEA 效應指「親手投入製作後對成品的偏愛增加」。Jerry 想用此效應讓 Mia 多吃蔬菜，最有效是「讓 Mia 一起參與烹煮」，故 (B) Ask Mia to help him cook vegetables for her meal 正確。(A) 只是聲明；(C) 是獎勵機制；(D) 是迎合喜好，皆未涉及親身投入製作。

media.passage_text:
In the picture is a UK electricity worker in the 1970s.  Pictures like this appeared in
many newspapers at the time.  In 1972, electricity workers in the UK asked to be paid more,
and they decided to stop working until their wish was answered.  The government worried
that there wouldn’t be enough electricity for the country if these workers didn’t go to work,
so it agreed to give them the pay rise.  However, the story didn’t end there.
A year later, the electricity workers asked for another pay rise.  To get what they
wanted, they worked shorter hours and acted like they would walk away from their job
anytime.  But this time, the government decided to fight.  To save electricity, it made new
rules for people to follow.  For example, each family could turn on the heat in only one
room; TV stations had to stop their shows at 10:30 p.m.; businesses could open only three
days a week.
People’s lives were greatly changed.  Without enough heat to keep the house warm,
people covered themselves in blankets all the time.  Hospitals had to use candles because
they couldn’t keep all the lights on.  Factories couldn’t keep their machines working.  Many
people lost their jobs.   For months, people were paying the price while the electricity
workers were fighting for their wish.  In the end, the government agreed to the second pay
rise.  This picture shows what many people thought of the electricity workers during the
“dark” time.
 electricity 電力 government 政府

questions.question_text: What is the reading mainly about?
options: The tips on using a picture to tell a story.
The change that electricity brought to people’s lives.
The history behind the picture of a UK electricity worker.
The story of a famous UK electricity worker from the 1970s.

source_meta.raw_text:
35. What is the reading mainly about?
    (A) The tips on using a picture to tell a story.
    (B) The change that electricity brought to people’s lives.
    (C) The history behind the picture of a UK electricity worker.
    (D) The story of a famous UK electricity worker from the 1970s.

questions.explanation: 全文以「1970 年代英國電力工人罷工照片」為核心，敘述照片背後的兩次罷工與政府應對的歷史，故 (C) The history behind the picture of a UK electricity worker 正確。(A) 是看圖技巧；(B) 是電力對生活影響；(D) 是個別工人故事，皆非主軸。

questions.question_text: In the UK in the 1970s, what did people most likely think of the UK electricity workers?
options: They were not brave enough to fix their problems.
They worked like robots and never learned to change.                   likely 可能
They were asking too much and did not know when to stop.
They did not care whether their job might hurt people’s health.

source_meta.raw_text:
36. In the UK in the 1970s, what did people most likely think of the UK electricity workers?
    (A) They were not brave enough to fix their problems.
    (B) They worked like robots and never learned to change.                   likely 可能
    (C) They were asking too much and did not know when to stop.
    (D) They did not care whether their job might hurt people’s health.

questions.explanation: 原文「For months, people were paying the price while the electricity workers were fighting for their wish」加上民眾因停電生活困頓，可推當時人們認為工人「索求過多、不知節制」，故 (C) They were asking too much and did not know when to stop 正確。(A) 不是不夠勇敢；(B) 不是死板；(D) 健康問題未在此被指責。

questions.question_text: Why does the writer put the “ ” mark around the word dark in the last sentence?
options: To say that the “dark” time was actually not dark.
To tell people that the word was said by the government.
To mean both the days without lights and the difficult lives people lived.
To show that the government and the electricity workers both lost their fights.

source_meta.raw_text:
37.   Why does the writer put the “ ” mark around the word dark in the last sentence?
      (A) To say that the “dark” time was actually not dark.
      (B) To tell people that the word was said by the government.
      (C) To mean both the days without lights and the difficult lives people lived.
      (D) To show that the government and the electricity workers both lost their fights.

questions.explanation: 「dark」加引號的雙關用法，既指字面缺電的黑暗，又指人們生活上的艱困期，故 (C) To mean both the days without lights and the difficult lives people lived 正確。(A) 否定字面意義不符；(B) 並非引述政府用語；(D) 兩方輸贏未在此句點出。

media.passage_text:
Most kids want gifts from their parents on their birthdays, but Cameron doesn’t.  On his
birthday, he gives his mom a gift.  He 38 doing this in his first year of junior high
school.  That year, during one class, he watched a video about how a baby is pushed from its
mother’s body.  After class, 39 .  How brave a woman is to have a baby!  Thank God I’ll
never have to do that!
When Cameron came home that day, he held his mom in his arms for a long time.  He
decided that his birthday 40 .  During those nine months inside his mom, he just ate and
slept while his mom was doing all the hard work.  So why should he get a birthday gift for
doing nothing?  If anyone should get a gift on his birthday, it should be his mom.
The 41 “birthday gift” Cameron prepared was a bag of cookies he baked.  The cookies
tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that 42 .
It was so much better than getting a gift.  Now, on his birthday every year, Cameron makes
a gift for his mother to thank her for giving him life.
This year, Cameron 43 his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make
dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and
hanging behind Cameron’s bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how
much he loves her.
